name: Early Blight
description: Early blight is a common fungal disease affecting tomato plants, caused by Alternaria solani. It appears as dark spots with concentric rings on lower leaves.
symptoms: Dark brown spots with concentric rings on leaves
Dark lesions on stems and fruits
spread: Spreads through wind, rain splash, contaminated tools, and infected plant residue in soil.

name: Late Blight
description: Late blight is a devastating disease caused by Phytophthora infestans, responsible for the Irish potato famine. It spreads rapidly in cool, wet conditions.
symptoms: White fungal growth on leaf undersides
spread: Spores spread via wind over long distances, rain splash, and contaminated equipment.

name: Early Blight
description: Early blight on potato causes characteristic bull's-eye lesions on foliage, reducing photosynthesis and tuber quality.
symptoms: Yellowing and wilting of lower leaves
spread: Persists in soil on infected debris; spreads via wind-blown spores and irrigation water.

name: Wheat Rust
description: Wheat rust is a fungal disease that appears as reddish-brown pustules on leaves and stems, significantly reducing grain yield.
spread: Urediniospores spread via wind over hundreds of kilometers; survives on alternate hosts.

name: Rice Blast
description: Rice blast is one of the most destructive rice diseases worldwide, caused by Magnaporthe oryzae, affecting all above-ground parts.
spread: Conidia spread through wind and rain splash; survives on rice stubble and weeds.

name: Gray Leaf Spot
description: Gray leaf spot is a foliar disease of maize caused by Cercospora zeae-maydis, reducing photosynthetic area significantly.
symptoms: Lesions run parallel to leaf veins
spread: Spores spread short distances by wind and rain; overwinters in corn residue.

name: Bollworm Damage
description: Cotton bollworm (Helicoverpa armigera) larvae feed on squares, flowers, and bolls, causing direct yield loss.
spread: Adult moths migrate long distances; multiple generations per season increase damage.

name: Bacterial Spot
description: Bacterial spot affects pepper leaves and fruits, caused by Xanthomonas species, reducing marketable yield.
spread: Spreads via rain splash, workers, tools, and contaminated seed.

name: Powdery Mildew
description: Powdery mildew on grape is caused by Erysiphe necator, appearing as white powdery coating on leaves and berries.
spread: Ascospores released in spring; conidia spread by wind throughout the season.

name: Apple Scab
description: Apple scab is a fungal disease caused by Venturia inaequalis, producing olive-green spots on leaves and fruit.
spread: Ascospores released from fallen leaves during spring rains; spreads via wind and rain.

name: Anthracnose
description: Mango anthracnose caused by Colletotrichum gloeosporioides affects flowers, leaves, and fruits, causing significant post-harvest losses.
symptoms: Black spots on leaves and fruits
spread: Conidia spread by rain splash and wind; survives on infected twigs and fallen leaves.

name: Sigatoka Leaf Spot
description: Sigatoka (Black Sigatoka) is a serious banana disease caused by Mycosphaerella fijiensis, reducing photosynthesis and yield.
symptoms: Dark streaks on leaves progressing to large spots
spread: Ascospores and conidia spread via wind and rain over long distances.
